title: Thelypteris oligophlebia
taxonomy: Thelypteris oligophlebia is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It is native to Central and South America. Thelypteris oligophlebia is classified as a member of the genus Thelypteris, which is a group of deciduous and evergreen ferns. The family Thelypteridaceae is a family of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or autumn ferns. Thelypteris oligophlebia is further classified within the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns that are known for their divided leaves.

intro: Thelypteris oligophlebia, also known as Maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Bioactive constituents of Thelypteris oligophlebia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
